items.mistake: Calling list() on an infinite itertools source like count() or cycle().
items.fix: Bound it with islice or takewhile before materializing.
items.why: Infinite iterators never finish and will hang or exhaust memory.
items.example:
```python
# BAD: hangs forever
list(itertools.count())

# GOOD: take just 10
list(itertools.islice(itertools.count(), 10))
```

items.mistake: Reusing an itertools chain/islice result after it's consumed.
items.fix: Recreate it, or store the items in a list if you need multiple passes.
items.why: These are one-pass iterators; a second loop yields nothing.
items.example:
```python
slice1 = islice(stream, 100)
for x in slice1: process(x)
for x in slice1: do_again(x)   # silent no-op — slice1 is exhausted
```

items.mistake: Applying lru_cache to a function with side effects or random/time-based output.
items.fix: Only cache pure functions whose result depends solely on arguments.
items.why: Memoization returns stale results and hides the side effects.

items.mistake: Expecting groupby to group non-adjacent equal keys.
items.fix: Sort by the key first; groupby only groups consecutive items.
items.why: groupby starts a new group whenever the key changes.
items.example:
```python
# items = [('a',1),('b',2),('a',3)]
groupby(items, key=lambda x: x[0])   # WRONG — three groups: a, b, a
groupby(sorted(items), key=lambda x: x[0])   # RIGHT — two groups: a, b
```
